response:
given an integer array nums sorted in non-decreasing order build and return an integer array result with the same length as nums such that result[i] is equal to the summation of absolute differences between nums[i] and all the other elements in the array however for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference  

for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference  

the first element in the array is at index 0 which is not a multiple of 3 so it is calculated as the summation of absolute differences between nums[0] and all other elements in the array  

the second element in the array is at index 1 which is not a multiple of 3 so it is calculated as the summation of absolute differences between nums[1] and all other elements in the array  

the third element in the array is at index 2 which is a multiple of 3 so it is calculated as the summation of square of the differences between nums[2] and all other elements in the array  

the fourth element in the array is at index 3 which is not a multiple of 3 so it is calculated as the summation of absolute differences between nums[3] and all other elements in the array  

the fifth element in the array is at index 4 which is not a multiple of 3 so it is calculated as the summation of absolute differences between nums[4] and all other elements in the array  

***  

for example in the first example nums is [2,3,5] the result array is [14,3,5] explanation result[0] is the sum of absolute differences between 2 and all other elements in the array so |2-2|^2 + |2-3|^2 + |2-5|^2 = 0 + 1 + 9 = 10 result[1] is the sum of absolute differences between 3 and all other elements in the array so |3-2| + |3-3| + |3-5| = 1 + 0 + 2 = 3 result[2] is the sum of square of the differences between 5 and all other elements in the array so |5-2|^2 + |5-3|^2 + |5-5|^2 = 9 + 4 + 0 = 13 but the output is 5 which indicates that
